steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content:
The angles of a triangle add to $180^\circ$. Since angle $G$ measures $112^\circ$, the other two angles must add to $68^\circ$.
$$180^\circ-112^\circ=68^\circ$$

steps.title: Find the possible measure
steps.content: Angle $H$ must be greater than $0^\circ$ and less than $68^\circ$, because angle $I$ also needs a positive measure. Among the choices, only $67^\circ$ is less than $68^\circ$. The answer is D.
